examples: Map scene: 'Something eased around Jaina's mouth.' -> 'The line of Jaina's mouth eased.'
Vereesa aftermath: 'something dry enough to keep the blood out of it' -> 'the old dry way' to keep Sylvanas terse instead of authorial.
Vereesa departure: cut 'The thing Sylvanas had expected did not happen' so the scene lands on Vereesa not looking back.
observations: Opening camp inventory stays in Sylvanas lane: horses, tack, feed, arrows, and map before any emotional summary.
Jaina reaching papers before food carries chapter18 residue cleanly and keeps the lean thriller register intact.
The camp-table exchange uses short tactical prompts and answer-lanes that match the style target's competence pressure.
The Jaina mouth-line was tightened from vague abstraction to a visible facial change.
Sylvanas's near-answer to Vereesa was pared back so the line sounds like her discipline, not editorial cleverness.

examples: Sending-stone scene: 'A different line ... Jaina had opened this one' -> 'Amber, not the colder blue Antonidas had left...' to avoid claiming more than Sylvanas can know.
Post-call read: 'there was nothing on her face Sylvanas could name...' -> 'her mouth had gone thin and her jaw was set too hard' to keep the read on visible evidence.
observations: Map assessment stays filtered through Sylvanas's route-reading and force-projection logic.
Vereesa is read through gait, title, grip, and fatigue; the chapter does not trespass into her interior.
The Antonidas call remains light, posture, and timing only, per the plan constraint.
The only real overreach risk in the call section was initiation certainty; that was trimmed back.
Closing travel pages keep Jaina's resonance legible through speech and body response, not omniscient explanation.

notes: Stratholme remains a horizon stain and residue source, matching chapter18 and the location memory file.
Lor'themar arrives only through spent political capital, stripped wit, and numbers, which matches his established decline.
The partnership reads as chosen formation and operational shorthand, not sudden thaw or confession.
observations: Opening smoke, cold ash, and camp order all continue chapter18's south-road aftermath rather than resetting it.
North is treated as the evolved answer-space from chapter18, never as a fresh revelation.
Vereesa carries the recall in the family-and-institution blend the plan required.
Lor'themar's off-page condition aligns with chapter14 memory: he is down to records, votes, and political spend.
The relay line ending at the harbors and Jaina falling into Sylvanas's left-rear lane both match locked continuity.

examples: Night ride sentence: 'became touch more than sight' -> 'became more touch than sight' to restore cadence and sense.
observations: The opening alternates a long stock-taking sentence with short camp anchors; it breathes the way the style guide wants.
Jaina's 'Grain carries. Bodies keep.' run is deliberate clipped theorem-language and earns its hammering rhythm.
Vereesa's 'Just numbers. Names. Dates.' passage lands as pressure-beat repetition, not accidental flatness.
The only line that actually stumbled on the ear was the travel sentence with the missing comparative; that was fixed.
The closing road paragraphs keep long/short variation by alternating terrain sentences with blunt directional beats.

notes: No causal gaps surfaced between map scene, recall arrival, Jaina's severance, Vereesa's departure, and the northbound launch.
The chapter bridge from chapter18 is fully honored: south is temporary road, north is the real pressure.
No scene break markers were needed or added; the chapter flows as continuous pressure.
continuity.crossChapter: Chapter 18 smoke, south-road residue, and the unspoken northward pull all carry directly into the camp opening.
observations: Scene 1 turns smoke and camp order into map work by causal pressure, not time stamp.
The bird-call brings Vereesa into camp exactly when the north decision hardens, so the interruption is earned.
Jaina's stone scene mirrors Sylvanas's refusal without breaking POV or emotional carryover.
Vereesa's exit leaves enough dust and silence in the air that Jaina's return does not feel emotionally reset.
The final ride establishes coast, failing relays, and ship pressure as direct handoff material for chapter20.

examples: Cut the anticipatory line before 'Vereesa did not look back' so the departure lands without narrator stage-setting.
Replaced the vague facial abstraction around Jaina's mouth with a specific visible change.
Flattened the 'keep the blood out of it' phrasing into cleaner Sylvanas register.
observations: The opening avoids scene-throat-clearing; it starts on Stratholme's stain and the camp's practical order.
No biography dump was reintroduced when Vereesa reports Lor'themar; the scene stays on immediate cost.
The worst surviving abstraction was the mouth-easing sentence; it is now concrete.
The dry-answer line was the strongest leftover trace of writerly commentary; it is now cleaner.
The departure paragraph no longer announces its own effect before giving it.

notes: No spoken-line contamination from hitlist terms such as vector, actionable, optimal, or ingested.
Jaina stays in field-plain language even when describing the deeper plague mechanism.
The formal recall reads as document language on purpose because Vereesa is quoting the chamber order.
observations: Jaina's northbound explanation stays concrete: grain, bodies, coast, signal line, ship.
Sylvanas's dialogue remains clipped and tactical without lapsing into generic stoic monosyllables.
Vereesa's family plea uses rank, home, and name rather than bureaucratic abstraction.
Lor'themar's off-page report is carried through Vereesa in plain nouns—figures, names, dates, grain totals—not report-speak contamination.
The chapter's spoken language already lived below the plain-language ceiling, so this pass confirmed rather than rewrote it.
